RETURN.content: a set of all edges connecting source vertex to target vertex if such vertices exist in this graph. If any of the vertices does not exist or is null, returns null. If both vertices exist but no edges found, returns an empty set.

RETURN.content: an edge connecting source vertex to target vertex if such vertices and such edge exist in this graph; otherwise null

RETURN.description: true if this graph contains the specified edge.
RETURN.content: true if and only if this graph contains an edge going from the source vertex to the target vertex. In undirected graphs the same result is obtained when source and target are inverted. If any of the specified vertices does not exist in the graph, or if is null, returns false.

PARAM.description: edge whose presence in this graph is to be tested must not be null
PARAM.name: e
PARAM.content: edge whose presence in this graph is to be tested

RETURN.description: true if this graph contains the specified edge.
RETURN.content: true if this graph contains the specified edge. More formally, returns true if and only if this graph contains an edge e2 such that e.equals(e2). If the specified edge is null returns false.

PARAM.content: vertex whose presence in this graph is to be tested.

RETURN.description: a set of the edges contained in this graph.
RETURN.content: a set of the edges contained in this graph. The set is backed by the graph, so changes to the graph are reflected in the set.
